{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Experienced Real Estate Lawyer",
  "description": "Dillon Consultant Nigeria Limited is an architectural consultant/real estate development company located in Lagos, Nigeria &amp; The United States of America. Registered in February 1995. Dillon Consultant has as its primary focus - Property Development, Investment, Design Services and Facilities Management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Real Estate Lawyer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Role Description


	This is a full-time, on-site role for an Experienced Real Estate Lawyer located in Lekki, Lagos.
	The role involves managing real estate transactions, drafting and reviewing legal documents, providing expert legal advice on property laws, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
	The lawyer will collaborate with internal teams to oversee contracts, title verifications, and property transactions, ensuring legal and contractual soundness across all processes.
	Additionally, the role includes representing the company in legal proceedings related to real estate and managing client relationships effectively.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Law (LLB) and completion of Nigerian Law School (BL); membership in relevant professional bodies is an advantage
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in real estate law or a related field
	Proven record of managing real estate legal matters from preparation to completion.
	Strong knowledge of Property Law, Contract Law, and Corporate Law
	Expertise in drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts and legal documents
	Experience in title documentation, property registration, and conducting title searches
	Ability to provide comprehensive legal advice on regulatory compliance and risk mitigation
	Keen attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and excellent problem-solving abilities
	Effective communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills
	Proficiency in legal research and familiarity with real estate laws in Nigeria.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@dillonnigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Experienced Real Estate Lawyer Job at Dillon Consultant Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Dillon Consultant Nigeria Limited is an architectural consultant/real estate development company located in Lagos, Nigeria & The United States of America. Registered in February 1995. Dillon Consultant has as its primary focus - Property Development, Investment, Design Services and Facilities Management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Real Estate Lawyer

Job Location: Lagos

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a full-time, on-site role for an Experienced Real Estate Lawyer located in Lekki, Lagos.
	The role involves managing real estate transactions, drafting and reviewing legal documents, providing expert legal advice on property laws, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
	The lawyer will collaborate with internal teams to oversee contracts, title verifications, and property transactions, ensuring legal and contractual soundness across all processes.
	Additionally, the role includes representing the company in legal proceedings related to real estate and managing client relationships effectively.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Law (LLB) and completion of Nigerian Law School (BL); membership in relevant professional bodies is an advantage
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in real estate law or a related field
	Proven record of managing real estate legal matters from preparation to completion.
	Strong knowledge of Property Law, Contract Law, and Corporate Law
	Expertise in drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts and legal documents
	Experience in title documentation, property registration, and conducting title searches
	Ability to provide comprehensive legal advice on regulatory compliance and risk mitigation
	Keen attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and excellent problem-solving abilities
	Effective communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills
	Proficiency in legal research and familiarity with real estate laws in Nigeria.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

	Customer Service Officer is responsible for handling customer interactions, resolving complaints, providing product or service information, and ensuring a positive customer experience that supports business growth and retention.


Key Responsibilities

	Complaint Resolution

	Product Knowledge

	Problem-solving and conflict resolution ability
	Patience and emotional intelligence
	Basic computer skills (CRM systems, Microsoft Office)
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application Letter and CV to:&nbsp;info@janahluxurylimited.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Officer Job at Janah Luxury Limited




        
    

        
        Janah Luxury Limited is a Real Estate company located in Abuja Nigeria. Registered with The Coperate Affairs Commission in February 2022. Janah Luxury limited is into Real Estate and Construction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Customer Service Officer is responsible for handling customer interactions, resolving complaints, providing product or service information, and ensuring a positive customer experience that supports business growth and retention.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Customer Interaction
	Complaint Resolution
	Record Keeping
	Product Knowledge


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Minimum of OND/HND/Bachelor’s degree (varies by organization).
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Problem-solving and conflict resolution ability
	Patience and emotional intelligence
	Basic computer skills (CRM systems, Microsoft Office)
	Ability to multitask and work under pressure.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application Letter and CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Asset and End User Device Engineer",
  "description": "Vennote Technologies Limited is a well-established ICT company with experience spanning over two decades in enterprise solutions using best of breed products in very large IT deployments around the world. Vennote Technologies Limited has certified Skilled Technical resources delivering world class implementation and support services through the use of best practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;IT Asset and End User Device Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly skilled and proactive End User Device Engineer to join our IT team.
	You will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining the hardware, software, and configuration standards for end user devices across the company.
	This includes laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and peripherals across Windows and macOS environments.
	The ideal candidate is experienced in enterprise device management, automation, endpoint security, and end user experience optimization in a modern, hybrid work environment.


Key Responsibilities
Device Lifecycle Management:


	Manage the provisioning, configuration, deployment, and decommissioning of end user devices.
	Develop and maintain golden images and deployment workflows for Windows and macOS systems.
	Manage and monitor asset inventory systems and maintain accurate device records.


Endpoint Management:


	Administer MDM and endpoint management platforms such as Microsoft Intune.
	Automate device enrollment, software deployment, patch management, and compliance enforcement.
	Establish and enforce security baselines for endpoints (e.g., encryption, antivirus, firewall, device lock).


Automation &amp; Scripting:


	Develop and maintain automation scripts for device setup, software installations, and system monitoring using PowerShell, Bash, Python, or similar tools.
	Integrate device management with CI/CD pipelines where relevant (e.g., for automated testing environments).


Security &amp; Compliance:


	Collaborate with security teams to enforce endpoint security controls, including disk encryption (BitLocker, File Vault), antivirus, EDR, and MFA.
	Support compliance with industry standards such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR.
	Monitor and respond to endpoint-related vulnerabilities and incidents.


User Support &amp; Experience:


	Act as Tier 2 escalation point for device-related incidents and service requests.
	Collaborate with the Service Desk to document SOPs and knowledge base articles.
	Ensure a seamless user experience by monitoring performance and proactively addressing issues.


Software &amp; Licensing:


	Manage licenses for core productivity tools (e.g., Microsoft 365, Adobe Creative Cloud, Zoom).
	Ensure proper software licensing and compliance across the device fleet.


Project Participation:


	Lead or contribute to IT projects related to device refresh, remote work enablement, onboarding automation, and more.
	Evaluate and recommend tools and technologies to improve device management and user productivity.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience.
	3+ years of experience in end user device engineering, endpoint management, or related IT roles.
	Proficiency with intune MDM platform.
	Strong knowledge of Windows 10/11, macOS, and basic Linux systems administration.
	Experience with automation tools and scripting languages (e.g., PowerShell, Bash, Python).
	Familiarity with enterprise security standards and practices.
	Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and documentation skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to:&nbsp;dave.nwokedi@vennote.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&quot;EUD and IT Asset&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

IT Asset and End User Device Engineer Job at Vennote Technologies Limited




        
    

        
        Vennote Technologies Limited is a well-established ICT company with experience spanning over two decades in enterprise solutions using best of breed products in very large IT deployments around the world. Vennote Technologies Limited has certified Skilled Technical resources delivering world class implementation and support services through the use of best practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Asset and End User Device Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and proactive End User Device Engineer to join our IT team.
	You will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining the hardware, software, and configuration standards for end user devices across the company.
	This includes laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and peripherals across Windows and macOS environments.
	The ideal candidate is experienced in enterprise device management, automation, endpoint security, and end user experience optimization in a modern, hybrid work environment.


Key Responsibilities
Device Lifecycle Management:


	Manage the provisioning, configuration, deployment, and decommissioning of end user devices.
	Develop and maintain golden images and deployment workflows for Windows and macOS systems.
	Manage and monitor asset inventory systems and maintain accurate device records.


Endpoint Management:


	Administer MDM and endpoint management platforms such as Microsoft Intune.
	Automate device enrollment, software deployment, patch management, and compliance enforcement.
	Establish and enforce security baselines for endpoints (e.g., encryption, antivirus, firewall, device lock).


Automation & Scripting:


	Develop and maintain automation scripts for device setup, software installations, and system monitoring using PowerShell, Bash, Python, or similar tools.
	Integrate device management with CI/CD pipelines where relevant (e.g., for automated testing environments).


Security & Compliance:


	Collaborate with security teams to enforce endpoint security controls, including disk encryption (BitLocker, File Vault), antivirus, EDR, and MFA.
	Support compliance with industry standards such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR.
	Monitor and respond to endpoint-related vulnerabilities and incidents.


User Support & Experience:


	Act as Tier 2 escalation point for device-related incidents and service requests.
	Collaborate with the Service Desk to document SOPs and knowledge base articles.
	Ensure a seamless user experience by monitoring performance and proactively addressing issues.


Software & Licensing:


	Manage licenses for core productivity tools (e.g., Microsoft 365, Adobe Creative Cloud, Zoom).
	Ensure proper software licensing and compliance across the device fleet.


Project Participation:


	Lead or contribute to IT projects related to device refresh, remote work enablement, onboarding automation, and more.
	Evaluate and recommend tools and technologies to improve device management and user productivity.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience.
	3+ years of experience in end user device engineering, endpoint management, or related IT roles.
	Proficiency with intune MDM platform.
	Strong knowledge of Windows 10/11, macOS, and basic Linux systems administration.
	Experience with automation tools and scripting languages (e.g., PowerShell, Bash, Python).
	Familiarity with enterprise security standards and practices.
	Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and documentation skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using "EUD and IT Asset” as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales & Growth Manager",
  "description": "SmartStream is a recruitment and outsourcing firm focused on helping businesses find, verify, and manage the right talent. We deliver efficient, scalable workforce solutions that drive productivity and growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales &amp; Growth Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria - Remote
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	This is a performance-driven growth role.
	Responsibilities include daily cold outreach to salons &amp; nail techs, converting leads into free trial users, managing the waitlist pipeline, tracking performance metrics, ensuring premium marketing content quality, and reaching out to influencers for partnerships and live demos.
	You are helping build early momentum for a SaaS startup.


Key Responsibilities
Cold Outreach &amp; Lead Generation:


	Send 50&ndash;100 targeted DMs daily
	Follow up consistently
	Qualify serious prospects
	Direct interested businesses to landing page
	Book demo calls if required
	Maintain organized CRM


Conversion &amp; Trial Activation:


	Drive signups for 14-day free trial
	Promote Founding 50 Program
	Follow up with trial users
	Encourage testimonial videos
	Identify case studies


Growth Tracking &amp; Reporting:


	Track DMs sent, replies, signups, activations, and conversions
	Submit weekly performance reports
	Suggest improvement strategies


Content &amp; Brand Quality Oversight:


	Work with video editor
	Maintain brand aesthetic (Midnight Blue / Soft Gold / White)
	Review captions
	Ensure clean, premium content
	Identify influencers for collaborations


KPIs (Performance Metrics)


	1,500+ DMs per month
	10&ndash;20% reply rate
	100+ trial signups in 60 days
	30&ndash;50 Founding 50 conversions
	Weekly content review completed


Ideal Candidate


	Strong communication skills
	Experience in cold outreach
	Understands beauty/fashion industry
	Organized and data-driven
	Understands conversion psychology


Work Structure:


	Fully remote
	Daily activity tracking
	Weekly strategy meeting
	Opportunity to grow into Head of Growth


Compensation Structure


	Salary: N250,000 - N350,000 per month.
	Option B &ndash; Lower Base + Higher Commission
	Fixed monthly base ($X)
	Commission per paid subscriber ($X per conversion)
	Bonus at 50 paid users milestone
	Performance-based growth incentives.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to:&nbsp;smartstream.hires@outlook.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Sales & Growth Manager Job at SmartStream




        
    

        
        SmartStream is a recruitment and outsourcing firm focused on helping businesses find, verify, and manage the right talent. We deliver efficient, scalable workforce solutions that drive productivity and growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales & Growth Manager

Job Location: Nigeria - Remote
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a performance-driven growth role.
	Responsibilities include daily cold outreach to salons & nail techs, converting leads into free trial users, managing the waitlist pipeline, tracking performance metrics, ensuring premium marketing content quality, and reaching out to influencers for partnerships and live demos.
	You are helping build early momentum for a SaaS startup.


Key Responsibilities
Cold Outreach & Lead Generation:


	Send 50–100 targeted DMs daily
	Follow up consistently
	Qualify serious prospects
	Direct interested businesses to landing page
	Book demo calls if required
	Maintain organized CRM


Conversion & Trial Activation:


	Drive signups for 14-day free trial
	Promote Founding 50 Program
	Follow up with trial users
	Encourage testimonial videos
	Identify case studies


Growth Tracking & Reporting:


	Track DMs sent, replies, signups, activations, and conversions
	Submit weekly performance reports
	Suggest improvement strategies


Content & Brand Quality Oversight:


	Work with video editor
	Maintain brand aesthetic (Midnight Blue / Soft Gold / White)
	Review captions
	Ensure clean, premium content
	Identify influencers for collaborations


KPIs (Performance Metrics)


	1,500+ DMs per month
	10–20% reply rate
	100+ trial signups in 60 days
	30–50 Founding 50 conversions
	Weekly content review completed


Ideal Candidate


	Strong communication skills
	Experience in cold outreach
	Understands beauty/fashion industry
	Organized and data-driven
	Understands conversion psychology


Work Structure:


	Fully remote
	Daily activity tracking
	Weekly strategy meeting
	Opportunity to grow into Head of Growth


Compensation Structure


	Salary: N250,000 - N350,000 per month.
	Option B – Lower Base + Higher Commission
	Fixed monthly base ($X)
	Commission per paid subscriber ($X per conversion)
	Bonus at 50 paid users milestone
	Performance-based growth incentives.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Agidingbi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assist in end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding).
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases accurately.
